skill.name: meeting-minutes-assistant
skill.description: 会议纪要自动整理 — 录音/文字记录→结构化纪要+待办事项+责任人追踪
skill.summary: Automatically convert meeting transcripts into structured minutes with action items and task assignments
skill.author: Hermes-Sedimentary

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged two high-severity patterns: a weak cryptographic algorithm indicator at SKILL.md:4 and high file entropy (6.21 bits) at SKILL.md:1. Both were evaluated as false positives. The weak crypto flag triggered on a Chinese-language description string containing no cryptographic code. The high entropy is expected for CJK character content in a markdown file and does not indicate obfuscation. No actual security risks were identified; the skill is a text-processing instruction set with no network access, file system operations, code execution, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged line 4 as containing a weak cryptographic algorithm. Line 4 contains only a Chinese-language description string with no cryptographic code. This false positive was likely triggered by character pattern matching on CJK text that coincidentally resembles encoded data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct inspection of line 4 confirms it is a plain description string with no crypto-related code, making this an unambiguous false positive

low_findings.title: False Positive: High File Entropy Detection
low_findings.description: Static analyzer detected high entropy (6.21 bits) at line 1, suggesting possible obfuscation or encrypted content. The file contains Chinese characters which naturally have higher byte entropy than ASCII English text. This is a well-known false positive pattern in entropy-based detection for multi-byte character content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: CJK characters inherently produce higher Shannon entropy; the file is plain readable markdown with no evidence of obfuscation or encryption

content.user_title: Transform Meeting Transcripts into Structured Minutes
content.value_statement: Meeting notes are often disorganized and action items get lost in long transcripts. This skill automatically converts meeting recordings or text transcripts into structured minutes with clear action items, responsible persons, and key decisions.
actual_capabilities: Convert meeting transcripts into structured meeting minutes with clear sections
Extract action items and assign responsible persons from discussion text
Generate concise summaries of key discussion points and decisions
Track decisions made during meetings with supporting context
Process both formal transcripts and informal meeting notes as input
limitations: Requires clear audio transcripts or well-formatted text for best results
Cannot join live meetings or record audio directly
May miss nuanced context not explicitly stated in the transcript
Manual review recommended for critical business or legal decisions

use_cases.title: Team Standup Summaries
use_cases.description: Convert daily team standup notes into structured summaries with individual status updates, identified blockers, and consolidated action items.

use_cases.title: Client Meeting Documentation
use_cases.description: Transform client call transcripts into professional meeting minutes with deliverables, deadlines, and responsibility assignments.

use_cases.title: Project Review Notes
use_cases.description: Organize project review discussions into decisions, risks, next steps, and follow-up items grouped by topic area.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Meeting Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize this meeting transcript into key points, decisions, and action items.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick meeting recap when you need a simple structured summary

prompt_templates.title: Action Item Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all action items from this meeting notes. For each item, identify the responsible person, the task description, and any mentioned deadline.
prompt_templates.scenario: Following up after a meeting to ensure all tasks are captured and assigned

prompt_templates.title: Full Meeting Minutes Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Process this meeting transcript and create structured minutes with sections for: attendees, agenda items, discussion summary per topic, decisions made, action items with owners and deadlines, and items for next meeting.
prompt_templates.scenario: Formal meeting documentation that needs to be shared with stakeholders

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Speaker Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this multi-speaker meeting transcript. Identify each speaker's contributions, detect points of agreement and disagreement, extract all decisions with their rationale, and create a comprehensive action plan with clear ownership and timelines.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex meetings with multiple participants where tracking contributions matters

output_examples.input: Meeting transcript: Alice discussed Q3 budget, needs 10% increase for marketing. Bob agreed to review by Friday. Charlie raised concern about resource allocation.
output_examples.output:
Meeting Summary:

Discussion Points:
- Q3 budget review, marketing requesting 10% increase
- Resource allocation concerns raised

Decisions:
- Budget review to be completed by Bob

Action Items:
- Bob: Review Q3 budget proposal by Friday
- Alice: Provide detailed marketing budget breakdown
- Charlie: Document resource allocation concerns for next meeting

output_examples.input: Standup notes: Sarah working on login feature, blocked by API rate limiting. Mike completed payment integration, moving to notification system. Tom starting user profile page today.
output_examples.output:
Standup Summary:

Sarah: Working on login feature - BLOCKED by API rate limiting issue
Mike: Completed payment integration, now working on notification system
Tom: Starting user profile page implementation today

Action Items:
- Investigate API rate limiting solution for Sarah's blocker
- Mike to share payment integration handoff notes
- Tom to provide ETA for user profile page by EOD

best_practices: Provide clear speaker labels and timestamps in transcripts for accurate action item assignment
Include meeting agenda and context to improve summary relevance and completeness
Review all generated action items and responsible persons before distributing to the team
anti_patterns: Using generated minutes as legal or compliance documents without human review
Feeding heavily redacted or incomplete transcripts and expecting accurate results
Distributing auto-generated minutes without first verifying assigned responsibilities and deadlines

faq.question: What input formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill accepts meeting transcripts as plain text, including pasted notes, transcribed recordings, or structured meeting logs with speaker labels.

faq.question: Can it process audio recordings directly?
faq.answer: No, this skill works with text input. You should transcribe recordings first using a speech-to-text tool before processing the transcript.

faq.question: How accurate are the generated action items?
faq.answer: The skill identifies explicitly stated tasks and decisions from the transcript. Accuracy depends on input quality. Manual review of generated items is recommended.

faq.question: Does it support multiple languages?
faq.answer: The skill processes the language used in the input transcript. It works with any language supported by Claude, provided the text is clear and well-structured.

faq.question: Can I customize the meeting minutes format?
faq.answer: Yes, you can specify your preferred format in the prompt, including custom section headers, templates, or specific fields you want included in the output.

faq.question: Is my meeting data secure?
faq.answer: The skill processes text within your conversation session. No meeting data is stored or transmitted externally beyond the standard AI model processing pipeline.
